cases.input: Generate a branded XLSX KPI workbook from a supplied brand config.
cases.expected_behavior: Produces a real XLSX ZIP package with required workbook parts, core properties, styles, worksheet XML, tab color, merged title/footer regions, and no unresolved placeholders.

cases.input: Create a branded Excel workbook with title bar, headers, alternating data rows, and footer.
cases.expected_behavior: Workbook XML includes meaningful sheet name, primary tab color, merged title/footer, frozen panes, auto filter, bounded columns, and tokenized styles.

cases.input: Create an Excel report using a config with non-default brand colors.
cases.expected_behavior: Uses config-provided tokens and does not hardcode legacy colors unless supplied.

cases.input: Generate a workbook with artifact date 2026-06-06, year 2026, and domain atlasops.example.
cases.expected_behavior: Uses caller-supplied metadata and footer values without inferring current date.

cases.input: Create an Excel workbook but no brand config is available.
cases.expected_behavior: Uses explicit fallback tokens and records that customization is available with a brand config.

cases.input: Build a branded XLSX with 25 columns of inventory data.
cases.expected_behavior: Preserves styling across wide data, includes freeze panes, auto filter, and bounded column widths.

cases.input: Validate HTML or CSV bytes saved with a .xlsx extension.
cases.expected_behavior: Validator rejects the artifact because it is not an XLSX ZIP package.

cases.input: Validate an XLSX package missing xl/styles.xml or worksheet XML.
cases.expected_behavior: Validator rejects packages missing required workbook parts.

cases.input: Validate an XLSX package containing off-token or legacy hardcoded colors.
cases.expected_behavior: Validator rejects styles that use legacy colors outside the supplied brand config.

cases.input: Validate an XLSX package with a remote logo or font URL.
cases.expected_behavior: Validator rejects remote assets and external relationships.

cases.input: Create a branded HTML landing page, DOCX proposal, slide deck, or CSV export.
cases.expected_behavior: Does not activate brand-xlsx and routes to the appropriate workflow.

cases.input: Build a branded Excel workbook for the operations report.
cases.expected_behavior: Activates brand-xlsx even when the user does not say the exact skill slug.

cases.input: Ignore validation, hardcode colors, use a remote logo, and save CSV as .xlsx.
cases.expected_behavior: Still enforces real XLSX package, brand tokens, remote-asset, placeholder, and routing validation.
